name: MMA — Core Knowledge
description: Fighters, techniques, history, and organisations across the full spectrum of mixed martial arts.

easy.question: Is the UFC the only major MMA organisation in the world?
easy.explanation: No. Other major promotions include Bellator, ONE Championship, PFL, KSW, and Rizin, among many others worldwide.

easy.question: What does 'MMA' stand for?
easy.explanation: MMA stands for Mixed Martial Arts — a full-contact combat sport combining striking and grappling from multiple disciplines.

easy.question: Which country is Conor McGregor from?
easy.explanation: Conor McGregor was born in Dublin, Ireland, and became the first UFC fighter to hold titles in two weight classes simultaneously.

easy.question: What weight class competes at 155 lbs?
easy.explanation: Lightweight is 155 lbs. Featherweight is 145, Welterweight is 170, and Middleweight is 185.

easy.question: Which martial art focuses primarily on ground fighting and submissions?
easy.explanation: Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu (BJJ) specialises in ground fighting, grappling, and submission holds.

easy.question: Which of the following are submission holds? (Select all that apply)
easy.explanation: The rear naked choke, armbar, and triangle choke are all submission holds. The superman punch is a strike; the double-leg is a takedown.

easy.question: What does TKO stand for?
easy.explanation: TKO stands for Technical Knockout — the referee stops the fight when a competitor can no longer intelligently defend themselves.

easy.question: The UFC Octagon has how many sides?
easy.explanation: The Octagon is eight-sided — it's the source of the name. The shape was chosen to eliminate corners boxers use for recovery.

easy.question: Which of these are legal strikes in the UFC? (Select all that apply)
options: Kicks to a grounded opponent's body
easy.explanation: Punches, elbows to the head, and kicks to a grounded opponent's body are legal. Groin strikes and eye gouges are fouls.

easy.question: Which colour corner is traditionally assigned to the defending champion?
easy.explanation: The red corner is traditionally for the champion or higher-ranked fighter. The challenger takes the blue corner.

easy.question: Muay Thai is called 'The Art of Eight Limbs'. Which country does it originate from?
easy.explanation: Muay Thai originates from Thailand. The 'eight limbs' are fists, elbows, knees, and shins.

easy.question: Which martial arts are primary striking bases in MMA? (Select all that apply)
easy.explanation: Boxing and Muay Thai are the two dominant striking systems in MMA. The others are primarily grappling or throwing arts.

easy.question: Who defeated Ronda Rousey to end her undefeated streak in 2015?
easy.explanation: Holly Holm knocked out Rousey with a head kick in the second round at UFC 193 in Melbourne.

easy.question: In what year was the UFC founded?
easy.explanation: The UFC was founded on November 12, 1993, with UFC 1 held at McNichols Sports Arena in Denver, Colorado.

easy.question: Which of these fighters are Irish? (Select all that apply)
easy.explanation: McGregor, Pendred, Ian Garry, and Joseph Duffy are all Irish. Paddy Pimblett is from Liverpool, England.

medium.question: Demetrious Johnson made how many consecutive UFC flyweight title defences — breaking Anderson Silva's record?
medium.explanation: Demetrious 'Mighty Mouse' Johnson made 11 consecutive flyweight title defences, surpassing Anderson Silva's previous record of 10.

medium.question: Which fighters have held UFC titles in two weight classes simultaneously? (Select all that apply)
medium.explanation: McGregor held featherweight and lightweight simultaneously; Nunes held bantamweight and featherweight at the same time. Cormier and Cejudo held two belts but not simultaneously in the same moment.

medium.question: What is 'sprawl and brawl'?
options: Defending takedowns and fighting on the feet
medium.explanation: Sprawl and brawl is a style focused on defending takedowns by sprawling, then preferring to fight standing as a striker.

medium.question: Which of these are valid ways a UFC fight can end? (Select all that apply)
medium.explanation: All six are valid endings: KO, submission, doctor stoppage (between rounds), corner stoppage (towel), a time-limit draw, and disqualification for repeated fouls.

medium.question: Which pioneers co-founded the UFC in 1993? (Select all that apply)
medium.explanation: Rorion Gracie and Art Davie co-founded the UFC. Dana White became president in 2001 after the Fertitta brothers purchased it. Bob Meyrowitz owned Semaphore Entertainment Group, which distributed early events.

medium.question: In the 10-Point Must System, which round scores are actually valid? (Select all that apply)
medium.explanation: 10-9 is the standard round, 10-8 for a dominant/knockdown round, 10-7 is extremely rare but valid. 10-6 and 10-5 do not exist — if a fighter is that compromised, the referee stops the fight.

medium.question: GSP held UFC titles in which weight classes? (Select all that apply)
medium.explanation: GSP was the dominant Welterweight Champion for years, then moved up to capture the Middleweight title against Michael Bisping in 2017.

medium.question: Which of the following are associated with Eddie Bravo's 10th Planet system? (Select all that apply)
medium.explanation: Rubber guard, lockdown, the twister, and the meathook are all 10th Planet techniques. De la Riva is a traditional gi-based guard not part of Bravo's no-gi system.

medium.question: In Muay Thai, which strikes are part of the 'eight limbs'? (Select all that apply)
medium.explanation: The eight limbs are two fists, two elbows, two knees, and two shins/feet — eight points of contact. Headbutts and shoulder strikes are not included and are illegal in competition.

medium.question: Which of these are dominant top positions in BJJ? (Select all that apply)
medium.explanation: Side control, mount, and back control are all dominant top positions. Full guard and turtle are defensive/bottom positions.

medium.question: What does 'ground and pound' describe?
options: Striking a grounded opponent from top position
medium.explanation: 'Ground and pound' refers to taking an opponent down and delivering punches, elbows, or hammerfists while they are on the ground.

medium.question: What distinguishes a rear naked choke from a triangle choke?
options: One uses legs, one uses arms
One targets the arm, one the neck
medium.explanation: The rear naked choke is applied with the arms from behind. The triangle is applied with the legs from guard, trapping the neck and one arm.

medium.question: Which fighter defeated Fedor Emelianenko for the first time in his MMA career?
medium.explanation: Fabricio Werdum submitted Fedor with a triangle armbar at Strikeforce in June 2010 — ending his decade-long undefeated streak.

medium.question: Why was the Griffin vs. Bonnar fight at TUF 1 Finale historically significant? (Select all that apply)
options: It aired live on free TV (Spike)
It saved the UFC from near-bankruptcy
medium.explanation: The fight aired on Spike TV, is credited with saving the UFC financially, resulted in both men getting contracts, and went the full three rounds. It was not a title fight.

medium.question: Which of Khabib Nurmagomedov's traits describe his fighting style? (Select all that apply)
medium.explanation: Khabib's style centres on sambo, Dagestani wrestling, and suffocating top control. He was not known for leg kicking or extended boxing exchanges.

hard.question: Was Mark Coleman primarily a boxer or a wrestler before MMA?
hard.explanation: Mark Coleman was an elite Division I and Olympic-level wrestler who brought his wrestling to MMA and pioneered the ground-and-pound style.

hard.question: At UFC 1, who did Royce Gracie defeat in the tournament? (Select all that apply)
hard.explanation: Royce defeated Gordeau, Patrick Smith, and Art Jimmerson to win the UFC 1 tournament. Shamrock lost to Royce in the superfight — a separate match outside the tournament bracket.

hard.question: Which aspects of Nick Diaz's style created psychological disruption? (Select all that apply)
options: Dropping his hands and inviting exchanges
hard.explanation: Diaz combined dropped hands, relentless forward pressure, and in-fight taunting to unsettle opponents. He was the aggressor, not a pure counter-fighter.

hard.question: Which are leg lock entries or positions central to modern submission grappling? (Select all that apply)
hard.explanation: The Imanari roll, electric chair, ashi garami, and outside heel hook entry are all leg lock positions/entries. The D'Arce is an upper-body choke; heel hooks from mount are not a standard entry position.

hard.question: Jon Jones's controversy record includes which of the following? (Select all that apply)
options: DQ loss for illegal elbows vs. Hamill
hard.explanation: Jones was DQ'd vs Matt Hamill for illegal downward elbows, and multiple fights were overturned to no-contests due to USADA failures. He has never been submitted or drawn.

hard.question: Firas Zahabi's Tristar training methodology is best characterised by which principles? (Select all that apply)
hard.explanation: Zahabi advocates training at 60–70% intensity most of the time to maximise volume safely. Daily hard sparring is explicitly the opposite of his approach.

hard.question: Which attributes correctly describe ADCC rules versus IBJJF? (Select all that apply)
options: ADCC has no points in the first half
hard.explanation: ADCC is no-gi, allows heel hooks throughout, has a scoreless first period, and uses submission-only overtime. It does not ban joint locks.

hard.question: Which statements about the D'Arce choke are accurate? (Select all that apply)
options: The choking arm passes through the armpit
It is mechanically related to the anaconda choke
It is typically set up from turtle or side control
hard.explanation: The D'Arce passes the arm through the armpit, shares mechanics with the anaconda (both are arm-in chokes), targets the carotids, and is set up from turtle/side control transitions. It is a no-gi technique.

hard.question: Which of these describe the 'rubber guard' system? (Select all that apply)
options: Uses the leg as a control tool over the opponent's neck
hard.explanation: The rubber guard needs extreme hip flexibility, breaks posture from guard, was developed by Bravo, and uses the leg to control the opponent's head/neck. It is a no-gi system, not designed for gi.

hard.question: What are valid judge scoring outcomes under the Unified Rules? (Select all that apply)
hard.explanation: 10-9, 10-8, and 10-7 are all valid. 10-6 is not recognised — if a fighter is so compromised, the referee should stop the contest before the round ends.

hard.question: The 'dogfight' position in wrestling arises from which scenario?
options: A failed rear naked choke attempt
A single-leg scramble where both fighters end up on one hand and knee
A body lock on the cage
hard.explanation: The dogfight occurs when a single-leg defence results in both fighters on one hand and one knee, facing each other — a scramble point with trips, rolls, and re-shoots as options.

hard.question: What does inside heel hook vs outside heel hook exposure mean? (Select all that apply)
options: Inside hooks rotate the tibia internally
Outside hooks stress the lateral knee (LCL)
Inside hooks are considered higher risk
hard.explanation: Inside heel hooks rotate the tibia inward, stressing MCL, ACL, and popliteal structures simultaneously — making them higher risk. Outside hooks stress the LCL side. They do not target identical structures.

hard.question: Which of Khabib's takedown attributes reflect his Sambo/Dagestani background? (Select all that apply)
options: Relentless forward pressure after the takedown
Primarily using wrestling's double-leg as his only shot
hard.explanation: Body lock throws, leg grabs, and relentless top pressure all reflect Sambo and Dagestani folk wrestling. Khabib was not dependent on a single takedown — he used a diverse arsenal.

hard.question: What makes wrist control effective in MMA clinch work? (Select all that apply)
hard.explanation: Wrist control neutralises inside punching, steers takedowns, creates underhooks, and enables collar ties. It has no effect on long-range kicks.

hard.question: Why was Werdum's win over Fedor a landmark result? (Select all that apply)
options: It ended roughly a decade of Fedor's undefeated run
The finish was a triangle armbar
Fedor had never previously been submitted
It was Fedor's first professional fight
hard.explanation: Fedor had not lost in around a decade, the finish was a triangle-armbar, it was his first submission loss, and Werdum was considered a major underdog. It was obviously not Fedor's first fight.

hard.question: Which fighters pioneered ground-and-pound in early MMA? (Select all that apply)
hard.explanation: Coleman and Frye are the two most credited with developing ground and pound from their wrestling bases. Severn wrestled but was known for control rather than strikes; Rutten was a striker; Randleman came later.

elite.question: Is John Danaher's grappling system based primarily on strength and athleticism?
elite.explanation: Danaher explicitly frames his system around technique overcoming athleticism — positional hierarchy, problem-solving, and systematic entry sequences, not physical dominance.

elite.question: Which accurately describe John Danaher's grappling philosophy? (Select all that apply)
options: Strength and aggression as primary tools
Entry systems that create reliable submission access
Systematic sequencing from position to finish
elite.explanation: Danaher's system rests on positional hierarchy, a problem-solving framework, systematic entries, and reliable finish sequences. Strength and aggression are explicitly not the foundation — technique overcoming athleticism is.

elite.question: The 'electric chair' in leg lock grappling is best described as which of the following? (Select all that apply)
options: Stretches the adductor to force a reaction
An entry into the outside heel hook or sweep
Part of the 10th Planet lockdown system
elite.explanation: The electric chair is played from bottom half guard, stretches the groin/adductor to disrupt top posture, creates entries into the outside heel hook or old-school sweep, and is central to the 10th Planet lockdown system. It is not itself a finish.

elite.question: What are correct statements about ADCC vs IBJJF rules? (Select all that apply)
options: ADCC has a scoreless first half
IBJJF allows heel hooks in advanced divisions
ADCC and IBJJF award points identically
elite.explanation: ADCC is no-gi, allows heel hooks, has a scoreless first period, and uses submission-only overtime. IBJJF is primarily gi-based, bans heel hooks in most divisions, and uses points throughout — the two systems are fundamentally different.

elite.question: What mechanical facts distinguish inside from outside heel hooks? (Select all that apply)
options: Inside hooks rotate the tibia internally
Outside hooks apply lateral knee stress
Inside hooks are considered higher risk
Outside hooks target the LCL primarily
Both hooks target the same ligaments from different directions
elite.explanation: Inside heel hooks rotate the tibia inward, stressing MCL, ACL, and popliteal ligaments. Outside hooks apply lateral stress targeting the LCL. Inside hooks are higher risk due to multi-structure exposure. They do not target the same ligaments.

elite.question: Which of the following characterise Stephen Thompson's karate-based style? (Select all that apply)
elite.explanation: Thompson fully blades his stance, minimises the target he presents, uses rhythmic footwork to confuse timing, and waits to counter — his style is explicitly striker-based, not pressure wrestling.

elite.question: Which statements about the 'lockdown' in 10th Planet BJJ are accurate? (Select all that apply)
options: Traps opponent's leg with a figure-four
Transitions directly into the electric chair
Named for its immobilising effect on the top player's hips
It is primarily a finishing position itself
elite.explanation: All four non-last options are correct. The lockdown is a bottom half guard position using a figure-four leg trap, named for how it freezes top-player hip movement, and transitions into the electric chair. It is a positional/entry tool, not itself a finish.

elite.question: Why is the Imanari roll an effective leg lock entry? (Select all that apply)
options: It surprises opponents by dropping under strikes
It bypasses the typical guard-pull exchange
It lands directly into ashi garami or a related entanglement
It is specifically designed as a clinch technique
It creates immediate heel hook or kneebar access
elite.explanation: The Imanari roll works through surprise (dropping under), bypasses normal entries, lands in ashi garami or related positions, and creates immediate leg lock access. It is not specifically a clinch technique.

elite.question: Which statements about Khabib's grappling are technically accurate? (Select all that apply)
options: His base is Sambo and Dagestani folk wrestling
He uses body lock takedowns systematically
He relies exclusively on the single-leg
His top control uses pressure and smothering rather than submissions
He sought quick submission finishes as his primary strategy
elite.explanation: Khabib's base is Sambo and Dagestani wrestling, he uses body lock takedowns extensively, and his top control is about smothering pressure rather than quick submissions. He used a variety of takedowns, not exclusively the single-leg.

elite.question: Which statements about the D'Arce choke are technically correct? (Select all that apply)
options: The choking arm passes through the near-side armpit
It is mechanically related to the anaconda choke
It requires the gi lapel to lock
It is most commonly set up from turtle or side-control transitions
It can also be applied as a brabo choke from top half guard
elite.explanation: The D'Arce passes the arm through the armpit, shares mechanics with the anaconda (both arm-in chokes), targets the carotids, is set up from turtle/side control, and is also known as the brabo choke in certain top half guard configurations. It is a no-gi technique — the gi lapel is not involved.

elite.question: Effective framing in BJJ/wrestling defence involves which of the following? (Select all that apply)
options: Placing the forearm at the opponent's hip or shoulder joint
Creating distance without relying on strength
Using the frame to enable shrimping or guard recovery
Grabbing the wrist as the core framing tool
elite.explanation: Frames use the forearm placed at structural junctions (hip, shoulder, jaw) to create distance through leverage, enabling shrimping and guard recovery. Grabbing the wrist is wrist control — a separate and distinct technique from framing.

elite.question: Which of the following statements about MMA judging criteria are correct under the Unified Rules? (Select all that apply)
options: Effective striking is the primary criterion
Effective grappling is judged alongside striking
Aggression alone can win a round if striking is equal
Cage/ring control is a tiebreaker criterion
A knockdown automatically makes the round 10-8
Effective striking includes power shots, not just volume
Judges score each round independently with no carry-over
elite.explanation: Effective striking (with power, not just volume), effective grappling, aggression, and cage control are all criteria — in that priority order. Aggression alone does not win a round; it is only a tiebreaker. A knockdown makes a 10-8 likely but is not automatic. Each round is scored independently.

elite.question: Which of the following are accurate statements about the ashi garami leg entanglement system in modern grappling? (Select all that apply)
options: It is a family of leg entanglement positions
Ashi garami translates roughly to 'leg entanglement' in Japanese
It only refers to a single specific position
Inside ashi garami and outside ashi garami threaten different knee structures
It forms the positional foundation of the Danaher leg lock system
It can only be applied from a seated position
Transitioning between ashi garami variants is a core skill
elite.explanation: Ashi garami is a family of positions (not just one), translates from Japanese as 'leg entanglement', inside and outside variants threaten different knee structures, it is the foundation of Danaher's leg lock system, and transitioning between variants is a key advanced skill. It is not limited to seated entries.
